title: Rate Limiting
description: Per-route and global rate limiting with extended lockout support

blocks.text: Rate limiting in Howl is built into the API pipeline. Every API route can have its own limit, fall back to a global default, or opt out entirely.
Limits are tracked per user ID (authenticated) or IP address (anonymous), using the cache adapter you configure.
Set defaultRateLimit in defineConfig to apply a limit to every endpoint unless overridden.
Override the limit on any individual route. A stricter or looser limit replaces the default entirely for that route.
By default, a blocked client is locked out for the remaining window time. For sensitive endpoints like login or OTP verification, use blockDurationMs to extend the lockout beyond the window.
When a client exceeds the limit, responses include Retry-After, X-RateLimit-Limit, and X-RateLimit-Remaining headers.
With multiple instances behind a load balancer, rate limit counters must be stored in a shared backend — otherwise each instance counts independently and the effective limit is max × N instances.
If you only set cache (not rateLimitCache), the same adapter is used for both. With memoryCache(), rate limits are per-instance and not enforced globally.
Howl automatically sets standard rate limit headers on every API response:

rows: Maximum requests allowed in the window
Requests remaining in the current window
Seconds until the block expires (on 429 only)
